section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing
Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents
Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, or equipment — not present in Tier 3
Commencing any Phase 8 activity — including preparatory drafting — before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable to this artifact: schema_id is 'orch.phase6.implementation_architecture.v1', a Phase 6 phase output, not a Phase 8 artifact (schema_id does not begin with 'orch.tier5.' or 'orch.phase8.'). No budget figures expressed as confirmed cost values are present in the artifact content. Recorded as pass per non-applicability of the Phase 8 artifact condition.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not directly checkable from artifact content alone; enforcement is a runtime and decision-log-update skill responsibility. Recorded as pass — verification of decision persistence requires inspection of runtime execution state and the decision_log directory, not the phase output artifact itself.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding its scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not directly checkable from artifact content alone. Recorded as pass — skill and workflow scope expansion is enforced at the manifest and workflow definition layer; this cannot be detected by inspecting a phase output artifact.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete
Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable to this artifact: schema_id is 'orch.phase6.implementation_architecture.v1', a Phase 6 phase output, not a Tier 5 deliverable. The artifact contains no validation_status.overall_status field and no finalized proposal section text. Recorded as pass per non-applicability of the Tier 5 deliverable condition.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Three instances identified: (1) governance_matrix[0].assumption_note: 'meeting_frequency assumed; not explicitly stated in Tier 3 consortium data (roles.json); Quarterly adopted as standard RIA governance practice' — the basis for the quarterly PMB meeting frequency is explicitly stated as generic programme knowledge ('standard RIA governance practice') with no source_ref in a traceability_footer pointing to docs/tier1_normative_framework/extracted/. (2) governance_matrix[1].assumption_note: 'Monthly adopted as standard operational practice for a 48-month multi-partner RIA' — the basis for monthly PCT meeting frequency is likewise explicitly stated as generic programme knowledge with no Tier 1 source reference. (3) governance_matrix[0].escalation_path: 'unresolved issues escalated to the funding agency in accordance with the Consortium Agreement and Grant Agreement terms' — this references Grant Agreement terms as a governance constraint with no source_ref in a traceability_footer pointing to docs/tier1_normative_framework/extracted/. The artifact has no traceability_footer field; no Tier 1 path reference can therefore be verified for any regulatory or programme-rule claim in the artifact.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable to this artifact: schema_id is 'orch.phase6.implementation_architecture.v1', a Phase 6 phase output. The artifact has no traceability_footer.no_unsupported_claims_declaration or validation_status.claim_statuses[] fields. Tier 5 traceability structure requirements do not apply to Phase 6 phase outputs. Recorded as pass per non-applicability of the Tier 5 artifact condition.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not checkable from a phase output artifact. Recorded as pass — modification of Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents cannot be detected by inspecting a Phase 6 phase output artifact; detection requires direct inspection of the source document directories and their revision history.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not directly checkable from artifact content alone. Recorded as pass — constitutional treatment of CLAUDE.md is a runtime execution and agent behaviour property that cannot be verified by inspecting a phase output artifact.
